evals.name: quest-completion-bug
evals.prompt: The 'Signierte Setliste' recipe works but the quest 'get_setlist_signed' never completes after combining the items. I think the flag is set but the quest stays active in the journal. Can you fix this?
evals.expected_output: A diagnosis identifying the missing completeQuest() call, a code fix using completeQuestWithFlag or explicit completeQuest, and a unit test. Should NOT introduce Pixi, i18n, or reference non-existent specialist skills.

assertions.text: Correctly identifies the missing completeQuest() or completeQuestWithFlag() call as the root cause
Proposed fix uses the actual Quest API (completeQuest, completeQuestWithFlag, or startAndFinishQuest) — not a fabricated method
Includes a unit test that validates the fix
Uses @/src/store (not @/store) for imports
Does NOT reference Pixi, i18n, game-balancing-assistant, audio-debugger-ambient-vs-gig, or other non-existent tools/systems

evals.name: new-crafting-recipe
evals.prompt: Add a new crafting recipe: combining 'Rostiges Plektrum' with 'Klebeband' should produce 'Repariertes Plektrum'. Also add a dialogue option in Proberaum where Matze mentions this combo if the player has both items.
evals.expected_output: A recipe added to the RECIPES array, a dialogue option in Proberaum with proper requiredItems check, correct import path (@/src/store), and an update note for dialog_uebersicht.md. Should NOT use wrong import paths or fabricated store fields.

assertions.text: Adds the recipe to the RECIPES array in src/store.ts (not some other location)
Recipe uses 'ingredients: [string, string]' field syntax, NOT 'item1'/'item2' fields
Dialogue option uses requiredItems (or equivalent item check) to gate on having both items
Mentions that dialog_uebersicht.md needs to be updated with the new dialogue and recipe
Includes a unit test for the new crafting recipe
Does NOT reference Pixi, i18n, state.player.money, or other non-existent fields/systems

evals.name: scene-wiring-bug
evals.prompt: I added a new scene called 'keller' but when I switch to it the player just falls through the floor and there's no ambient music. The scene file exists but something's not wired up right.
evals.expected_output: A diagnosis walking through the 6-step scene checklist, identifying which steps were missed (floor RigidBody, ambient track, Scene union type, Game.tsx wiring). Should reference actual conventions, not fictional ones.

assertions.text: References or walks through the scene addition checklist (or equivalent systematic check)
Identifies the missing floor RigidBody (type='fixed' at [0, -0.1, 0]) as the cause of falling through
Identifies the missing ambient track handling in audio.ts startAmbient() as the cause of no music
Checks whether 'keller' was added to the Scene union type in store.ts and wired in Game.tsx
Does NOT reference Pixi, i18n, or non-existent specialist skills in the diagnosis

evals.name: stale-closure-bug
evals.prompt: When the player interacts with the Wirt NPC in Kaminstube, the dialogue sometimes shows the wrong branch — it keeps showing the first-time greeting even though the player has already talked to him before. The flag 'wirtLegacy1982' is definitely being set. What's going wrong?
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis identifying stale closure capture in onInteract — the callback reads 'flags' from component render scope instead of useStore.getState(). Fix shows using useStore.getState() inside the callback. Should NOT suggest re-rendering tricks or external state managers.

assertions.text: Identifies stale closure capture as the root cause — the onInteract callback reads flags from render scope rather than useStore.getState()
Proposed fix uses useStore.getState() inside the onInteract callback to read current flag values
Does NOT suggest Pixi, i18n, external Redux, or other non-existent systems

evals.name: bandmood-overflow-bug
evals.prompt: I added a new interaction where the player earns +80 BandMood for finding a legendary guitar pick. But now when it triggers, the bandMood goes above 100 and the camera shake and music effects go haywire. How do I fix this so BandMood stays in range?
evals.expected_output: Identifies that direct set() calls don't clamp; recommends using increaseBandMood() which clamps automatically, or adding Math.max/min clamping. Should NOT recommend clamping in the UI layer or adding a watcher.

assertions.text: Identifies that the bug is caused by a direct set() or manual addition bypassing the clamp, not a UI issue
Recommends using increaseBandMood() (which clamps) or explicitly clamping in the mutator with Math.max(0, Math.min(100, ...))
Does NOT suggest Pixi, i18n, or external state managers
